items.title: Are We Paying For Health Care Or Hospitality? — HCP
items.summary: If medical tourism is profitable, does that additional revenue help moderate health care costs for Malaysians, or does it instead finance further investment in premium amenities, creating a continuous cycle of competition based on luxury rather than value? The post Are We Paying For Health Care Or Hospitality? — HCP appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Private Hospitals Say They’re At The Mercy Of Big Insurers
items.summary: Private hospitals say they're at the mercy of big insurance companies, with delisting threats if they don't give discounts at 20% to even 40%. APHM says medical equipment and nursing are actually undercharged to patients; running a hospital is very costly. The post Private Hospitals Say They’re At The Mercy Of Big Insurers appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Socso Says Paid Out RM1.2m In First Month Of Lindung 24 Hours
items.summary: Socso says it paid out over RM1.2 million under Lindung 24 Hours within the first month of implementation in June. The largest benefit payout was for implant costs at RM1.16 million, followed by over RM99,000 in temporary disablement benefit payments. The post Socso Says Paid Out RM1.2m In First Month Of Lindung 24 Hours appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: PH’s Johor Manifesto: RM100,000 Health Insurance Coverage For Private Hospitalisation
items.summary: PH's Johor election manifesto touts a free health insurance scheme with RM100,000 annual limit for 500,000 recipients among B40, M40, and select OKU to access private hospitals. Putrajaya's Base MHIT also has RM100,000 cover, but people must pay premiums. The post PH’s Johor Manifesto: RM100,000 Health Insurance Coverage For Private Hospitalisation appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Rewarding The Healthy: Why Health Assurance Needs Its Own No-Claims Discount — Dr Mohamed Rafick Khan
items.summary: A NCD model adapted for health offers a credible path to a pricing model that rewards good health outcomes rather than simply pooling everyone into the same cost curve. The post Rewarding The Healthy: Why Health Assurance Needs Its Own No-Claims Discount — Dr Mohamed Rafick Khan appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Redesign Base MHIT To Protect Senior Citizens
items.summary: The government should redesign the Base MHIT product in line with the PAC's recommendations, such as adopting community-rating, instead of risk-rating, to protect senior citizens. Premium schedules in Base MHIT policy contracts must be legally binding. The post Redesign Base MHIT To Protect Senior Citizens appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Are We Really ‘Over-Scoping’ Patients? — Dr Khairul Najmi Muhammad Nawawi
items.summary: Doing both OGDS and colonoscopy in one session is often the most efficient and appropriate approach. Comparing polypectomy rates between hospitals/countries without pathology and clinical context isn't meaningful. Stool tests are meant for asymptomatics. The post Are We Really ‘Over-Scoping’ Patients? — Dr Khairul Najmi Muhammad Nawawi appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why Malaysia Needs Stronger Laws Against Predatory Milk Marketing — June Joseph
items.summary: Protecting breastfeeding is one of the smartest investments Malaysia can make to reduce stunting, curb NCDs, strengthen human capital and secure the nation's future. The post Why Malaysia Needs Stronger Laws Against Predatory Milk Marketing — June Joseph appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MPs Demand Parliamentary Oversight Of Big Budget Cuts
items.summary: Some government and Opposition MPs back CodeBlue’s proposal for legislative or regulatory reform to require parliamentary approval or oversight in curbing the Executive's power to make large budget cuts, like 1% of a ministry's budget or another threshold. The post MPs Demand Parliamentary Oversight Of Big Budget Cuts appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: APHM Dismisses ‘Outdated’ PAC Report Amid Reset Initiative
items.summary: APHM says the PAC’s "really outdated" report doesn't take into account what was done in the last 18 months under Reset, adding that it's voluntary for both consumers and providers to participate in Base MHIT. DRG will be limited to Base MHIT too. The post APHM Dismisses ‘Outdated’ PAC Report Amid Reset Initiative appeared first on CodeBlue .
